text.title: testing-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for testing-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Module-level mutable fixture objects shared across tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any mutation by one test leaves the fixture in unexpected state for the next test, causing intermittent failures.

failure-mode.description: Asserting on mock call counts for behavior that is an implementation detail, not a business requirement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The test becomes coupled to the exact implementation, breaking on refactoring even when behavior is preserved.

failure-mode.description: Inverted pyramid: many E2E tests, few unit tests, no integration tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: E2E tests at 5 minutes each make CI prohibitively slow and provide poor diagnostic signal — a failure tells you something broke, not what or why. Unit tests at 50ms each provide fast, precise feedback and run before every commit.

failure-mode.description: Using text content or DOM structure as locators (e.g., `text=Login`, `div.form > button:first-child`).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These locators break on copy change or UI refactoring. data-testid attributes are stable by contract and immune to visual redesign.
